children.name: Paramythia
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Eastern Crested Berrypecker, a medium-sized, strikingly coloured passerine endemic to the montane forests of New Guinea. Its most distinctive feature is the tall, bushy crest that gives it a regal appearance. Males and females are similar in plumage, unlike the closely related Oreocharis, and feed primarily on soft fruits.

children.name: Oreocharis
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Tit Berrypecker, a tiny, active bird superficially resembling a tit or a white-eye, endemic to the mountains of New Guinea. It is the smallest member of the painted berrypecker family, with a short, fine bill and a tendency to forage acrobatically in the outer foliage. Its bright yellow breast makes it a striking sight in the moss forests.

children.name: Oreocharis arfaki
children.description: #REDIRECT Tit berrypecker Category:Taxa named by Adolf Bernhard Meyer.
